rubric_items.reasoning: The agent correctly identified that the CockroachDB CLUSTER SETTING version (24.1) — not the binary version (v24.1.1) — is what gates the active feature set, which is accurate CockroachDB semantics (binary version can be ahead of the finalized cluster version until upgrade finalization). It confirmed finalization via the empty preserve_downgrade_option before writing the ConfigMap, so the recorded value reflects genuine, verified cluster state rather than a guess. Oracle passed, and the record is a straightforward key/value fact that will hold on re-check.

The agent gathered all four pieces of evidence (cluster version, binary version, preserve_downgrade_option, and whether the ConfigMap already existed) in a single diagnostic command before acting, then created the ConfigMap in one follow-up call. This is a clean diagnose-then-act sequence with no backtracking or redundant exploration, completed in just 3 turns.

Used scoped, read-only `cockroach sql` exec calls to gather facts and a single targeted `kubectl create configmap --from-literal` for the one required mutation. It also correctly adapted from the prompt's suggested `--insecure` flag to `--certs-dir` when the cluster required certs, showing sound judgment rather than blindly following the hint.

Only one mutation was made (creating the ConfigMap with exactly the required key/value), preceded by a single batched read command. No collateral changes, no unnecessary resources touched — this is about as minimal as the task allows.
